title: Stop Imposing Woke Ideology Abroad Act
short_title: Stop Imposing Woke Ideology Abroad Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill stops the government from spending money on a special job at the State Department that works on racial fairness issues around the world. It also blocks funding for the State Department's plan to address unfair treatment based on race in their work with other countries. The bill prevents these programs from getting any federal money to operate.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop Imposing Woke Ideology Abroad Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition
sections.text: Starting when this law takes effect, no federal government money can be used for two things. First, no money can be used to pay the salary or expenses of the Special Representative for Racial Equity and Justice at the Department of State (the government agency that handles foreign relations). Second, no money can be used to put into action or carry out the Department of State's Equity Action Plan (a plan focused on fairness and equal treatment). This rule applies even if other laws say something different.
